topic: SAA African cell field intensity below 21,795 nT
our_argument_summary: Cycle 2 confirms: WIN-035 is trend-following retrodiction disguised as prediction. The 21,795 nT threshold is derived from WIN-005's CHAOS-7 extrapolation, not dome geometry. Both WMM2025 and the dome extrapolate the same INTERMAGNET station data (28-30 nT/yr decay). PROS-005 was registered 6 March 2026 and confirmed 12 March 2026 — a 6-day turnaround using publicly available WMM2025 data (released late 2024). The prediction has near-zero information content: given the established decay rate (~28-30 nT/yr), crossing 21,795 nT within 12 months is virtually guaranteed. Verdict: 'Std Model Explains' — non-discriminating trend extrapolation. Cycle 1 patches were largely applied; one patch (ISS-198) about the dome's claimed aetheric mechanism was incomplete.
cycle_1_review_status: Most fixes applied; one patch incomplete

ISS-150.description: WMM2025 relationship clarified: now says 'which synthesizes data from INTERMAGNET observatories worldwide' rather than listing separately

ISS-198.description: Proposed patch to add dome's claimed aetheric mechanism ('aetheric field geometry explains faster African cell decay...') was NOT included. Current text still says 'offers no mechanism' without engaging with the dome's actual claimed mechanism.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt — PROS-005 registered 2026-03-06, confirmed 2026-03-12 citing ~30 nT drop
WMM2025 release: late 2024/early 2025 (publicly available months before PROS-005 registration)
CHAOS-7 paleomagnetic model: African cell 23,050 → 21,880 nT (2000-2025) — source of 21,795 threshold
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: WIN-035 derives its 21,795 nT threshold from WIN-005's CHAOS-7 data extrapolation. The mechanism: (1) Observe African cell intensity decline from CHAOS-7 (23,050 → 21,880 nT over ~25 years). (2) Compute decay rate: (21,880 - 23,050) / 25 ≈ -46.8 nT/yr average, but dome sources cite ~28-30 nT/yr for recent decades (faster acceleration). (3) Extrapolate forward from 21,880 nT at rate of ~30 nT/yr → 21,880 - 30×1 ≈ 21,850 nT within a year. (4) Round to 21,795 nT as a threshold (slightly more aggressive). (5) Confirm by comparing WMM2025 output (~30 nT drop since January 2025) against threshold. This is observational curve-fitting, not dome geometric derivation.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Hardcoded threshold. The 21,795 nT value appears nowhere in dome source material as a derivation from disc_radius (20,015 km) or firmament_height (9,086 km). It is stated as 'derived from the WIN-005 exponential decay curve extrapolation.' The confirmation was done by manual comparison of WMM2025 output to the threshold (raw-text/02-wins.txt explicitly cites '~30 nT since January 2025'). No monitoring domain in monitor.py specifically tracks this threshold (as noted in Cycle 1 review: 'no domain in monitor.py references 21,795 nT or the African cell specifically').
data_sources_used: WMM2025 (publicly released late 2024, confirms ~30 nT drop since January 2025)
INTERMAGNET station data (underlying both CHAOS-7 and WMM2025)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: If the African cell intensity plateaued or rose, the dome could invoke 'aetheric damping equilibrium reaching a new metastable state' or 'measurement uncertainty masked by geomagnetic storms.' The 6-day registration-to-confirmation window suggests confirming data was already known.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. Cycle 1 review correctly identified this as non-monitored (no dedicated domain in monitor.py) and hardcoded confirmation. The patches applied in this cycle improved precision (28-30 nT/yr cited consistently, WMM2025 relationship clarified) but left one gap: the dome's claimed mechanism is still not engaged with directly.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No monitoring domain in monitor.py or pull_data.py tracks the 21,795 nT threshold or African cell intensity specifically. The Cycle 1 review documented that monitor.py Domain 8 ('SAA Decay Rate TTB') monitors general SAA decay at Tsumeb station for >=28 nT/yr (relevant to WIN-037), but this does NOT track WIN-035's specific African cell minimum intensity threshold. WIN-035 confirmation was performed manually: comparing WMM2025 output to the threshold via raw-text documentation. No automation exists for this specific WIN. Tagging as 'none'.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: SAA field intensity has been monitored by NOAA/NCEI (WMM series updated annually), ESA/Swarm (continuous satellite measurements), CHAOS-7 (paleomagnetic field model), and INTERMAGNET (ground-based observatories) for decades. The decay trend (African cell faster than American/Atlantic cell) was well-established by 2020. WMM2025 (released late 2024) already incorporated this trend. The dome model relabels the cause as 'aetheric field geometry' instead of 'reversed-flux patches in the outer core' but predicts no different numerical value or trajectory. The threshold itself (21,795 nT) is a reading-off of the observed trend — not a derivation.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: PROS-005 was registered 2026-03-06 and confirmed 2026-03-12 — a 6-day turnaround. The confirming data source (NOAA WMM2025) was publicly released in late 2024 and widely available months before registration. The threshold 21,795 nT was derived from CHAOS-7 (published data, available to anyone). The 6-day batch confirmation pattern is shared with WIN-035 through WIN-039 (all registered 2026-03-06, all confirmed within days using publicly available data sources). This indicates systematic after-the-fact data mining: the dome author reviewed what measurements had already been published, then registered predictions that would certainly be confirmed. Genuine prospective prediction would have required weeks or months of lead time to data availability.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The 21,795 nT threshold is explicitly stated as 'derived from the WIN-005 exponential decay curve extrapolation.' WIN-005 uses CHAOS-7 paleomagnetic observations (2000-2025), not dome geometric parameters. No formula in the dome repository connects disc_radius (20,015 km), firmament_height (9,086 km), sun_altitude (5,733 km), or any other dome geometric parameter to the value 21,795 nT. The dome's core parameters (from inject_ai_layer.py per CLAUDE.md) are fixed constants; no geometric derivation produces variable thresholds for different SAA cells. This is observational curve-fitting with an aetheric relabeling, not geometric prediction.

holes_found.description: Incomplete patch application for ISS-198. The decider proposed adding the dome's actual claimed mechanism ('aetheric field geometry explains faster African cell decay due to position relative to pole axis') to strengthen the rebuttal by showing we engaged with the dome's actual claim. This patch was proposed but not fully applied. The current text still says 'offers no mechanism' without noting what mechanism the dome actually claims.
holes_found.recommendation: Apply the remainder of ISS-198: update detail_evidence to explicitly state the dome's claimed mechanism ('aetheric field geometry predicts the African cell decays faster because of its position relative to the pole axis') before refuting it. This makes our argument more persuasive — showing we understand their claim before demolishing it.

holes_found.description: The 6-day registration-to-confirmation timing argument appears in detail_evidence but is stated passively ('indicating the confirming data was already in hand'). A dome defender would counter: 'The data was already available, but we didn't know it existed or what value it would show until we registered the prediction.' The argument needs more force: the batch pattern (5 predictions, same day, all confirmed within days with publicly available data) makes it clear that systematic after-the-fact data mining occurred, not prospective prediction.
holes_found.recommendation: Strengthen the timing argument in detail_evidence: 'The batch pattern is decisive: PROS-005 through PROS-009 (WIN-035 through WIN-039) were all registered 2026-03-06 and confirmed by 2026-03-12 using data sources (WMM2025, INTERMAGNET, published HeartMath records) available months earlier. This is not prospective prediction — it is systematic after-the-fact data mining with cosmetic time-lag framing.'

holes_found.description: Our finding statement says 'Non-discriminating: dome extrapolates same station trends as WMM2025; no unique prediction.' But this is weakly worded. We don't say WHAT is non-discriminating about it. A dome defender might argue: 'Of course our prediction agrees with WMM2025 — both are based on real data. That doesn't make it non-discriminating; that makes it correct.' We need to explicitly state that the prediction has near-zero information content: the threshold is set within the established trend, making confirmation virtually certain.
holes_found.recommendation: Expand finding to: 'Non-discriminating: dome extrapolates same station trends as WMM2025; threshold set within established trend guarantees confirmation; near-zero information content (99%+ probability of crossing 21,795 nT within 12 months given ~30 nT/yr established decay).'

holes_found.description: WIN-005 and WIN-035 are closely linked (WIN-035 derives its threshold from WIN-005's CHAOS-7 curve), but detail_evidence doesn't note this relationship. This creates a vulnerability: a dome defender could argue that WIN-005 was confirmed first, then WIN-035 extended it. The dependence relationship (WIN-035 threshold comes from WIN-005 data) is implicit but should be explicit.
holes_found.recommendation: Add to detail_evidence: 'WIN-035 is explicitly dependent on WIN-005: the threshold 21,795 nT comes directly from extrapolating WIN-005's CHAOS-7 curve. The two WINs should be read together.'

holes_found.description: Our detail_evidence says the dome 'offers no mechanism for why the decay rate is ~28–30 nT/yr rather than any other value.' We now know (from ISS-198 discussion) that the dome DOES claim a mechanism: 'aetheric field geometry predicts faster decay due to position relative to pole axis.' Our current text doesn't engage with this mechanism — it just asserts there is none. This weakens our rhetorical position.
holes_found.recommendation: Insert into detail_evidence: 'The dome claims "aetheric field geometry" explains why the African cell decays faster — citing its position relative to the pole axis — but provides no equations connecting this position claim to the observed 28-30 nT/yr rate, no prediction of when the decay will reverse, and no model of why different cell positions would produce different rates rather than identical rates.'

holes_found.description: The detail_extra field is null, but we could use it to note the overlap with WIN-037 and WIN-042 (all measuring SAA decay in slightly different framings). This would help readers see the WIN inflation pattern.
holes_found.recommendation: Set detail_extra to: 'Related observations: WIN-037 predicts 'field decay >=28 nT Since March 2025' (same timeframe, same decay rate); WIN-042 predicts 'Field decay >=28 nT/year' (same rate, different phrasing). WIN-035 measures African cell specifically; WIN-037 measures regional SAA; WIN-042 appears to be a global framing. All three use the same ~30 nT/yr decay trend, suggesting potential WIN count inflation (same observation framed three ways).'

stronger_arguments.description: The batch registration pattern is the most devastating argument. Five predictions (WIN-035 through WIN-039) all registered 2026-03-06, all confirmed within days using publicly available data released months earlier (WMM2025: late 2024, INTERMAGNET: continuous, HeartMath data: published). This is not prospective prediction — it is systematic data mining: review what measurements exist, then register predictions guaranteed to be confirmed, then mark them as 'confirmed' days later.
sources: raw-text/02-wins.txt: PROS-005 through PROS-009 all registered 2026-03-06, confirmed 2026-03-12
HeartMath data: published in peer review before 2026

stronger_arguments.description: The information content is near zero. The SAA African cell has been declining at 25-35 nT/yr for decades (CHAOS-7 shows this over 2000-2025). The threshold was set at 21,795 nT — just 85 nT below the last measured value (21,880 nT from 2025 CHAOS-7). At 30 nT/yr decay, crossing this threshold is virtually guaranteed within 3-4 months. This is like 'predicting' it will rain tomorrow in Seattle — the threshold is calibrated to produce near-certain confirmation.

The threshold (21,795 nT) has no derivation from dome geometry. It is stated as 'derived from WIN-005's exponential decay curve.' WIN-005 uses CHAOS-7 paleomagnetic observations. No formula in the dome repository connects disc_radius (20,015 km) or any geometric parameter to this specific value. The 'prediction' is reading off an observation and extrapolating it — what WMM2025 itself already does with quantified uncertainty bounds.
sources: WIN-035 claim text: 'derived from WIN-005 exponential decay curve'
raw-text/05-model.txt: no formulas linking 21,795 nT to geometric parameters
CLAUDE.md: dome core parameters (disc_radius, firmament_height, etc.) are fixed constants

stronger_arguments.description: The dome itself doesn't claim this as a prospective prediction — it's registered in the 'prospective weekly confirmations' section only after the data was already available. A genuine prospective test would specify the lead time and data release schedule. The fact that PROS-005 was confirmed 6 days after registration using data publicly available months earlier reveals the temporal sleight of hand.
sources: raw-text/02-wins.txt: '(Registered 2026-03-06, Confirmed 2026-03-12)' — but confirming data was from WMM2025 released late 2024
The category name itself is misleading: these are not prospective if confirmed with pre-existing data

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome model correctly identifies that the South Atlantic Anomaly (SAA) African cell is decaying faster than the Atlantic (American) cell, and that the field intensity in the African cell has reached historically low values. This is a genuine scientific observation — the SAA is one of the most important geomagnetic features of the current epoch. The recognition of asymmetric SAA cell behavior is real scientific awareness.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Observing a phenomenon is not predicting it. The asymmetry and decay rates were documented by NOAA, CHAOS-7, and INTERMAGNET well before PROS-005 was registered. The dome did not derive the 21,795 nT threshold from its geometry — it derived it by extrapolating the same CHAOS-7 data that standard models use. The 'prediction' amounts to reading a well-known trend forward by 3-4 months. WMM2025, IGRF-13, and standard geomagnetic models already do this with better quantified uncertainty estimates. A dome defender might argue: 'We correctly predicted the African cell would fall below 21,795 nT — that's a successful prediction.' The rebuttal is that any model which reads CHAOS-7 data and draws a line forward would 'predict' the same thing. This is not a test of the dome model — it is confirmation of the trend the dome merely repeated.

verdict_mismatch_check: No mismatch detected between verdict and current text. 'Std Model Explains' is well-supported. Both dome and WMM2025 extrapolate the same trend; no mechanism from dome geometry; post-hoc timing; near-zero information content. All point to 'Std Model Explains' (non-discriminating trend extrapolation).
deeper_analysis:
Cycle 2 review confirms all key findings from Cycle 1. WIN-035 exemplifies the dome model's 'prediction laundering' pattern:

**1. Observational Anchoring**
The dome does not derive the 21,795 nT threshold from its geometric parameters. It derives it by extrapolating CHAOS-7 paleomagnetic observations (23,050 → 21,880 nT over 2000-2025). This is legitimate scientific observation, but it is not prediction of the dome model's own making — it is adoption of pre-existing data.

**2. Trivial Threshold Within Established Trend**
The African cell has been declining at roughly 25-35 nT/yr for decades. The most recent CHAOS-7 value (2025) is 21,880 nT. Setting a threshold of 21,795 nT (85 nT below the last measured value) guarantees crossing within 3 months at 30 nT/yr. This is not a discriminating test — it is a threshold calibrated to ensure confirmation.

**3. Batch Registration After-the-Fact**
PROS-005 was registered 2026-03-06 and confirmed 2026-03-12. The confirming data (WMM2025) was released in late 2024. This is not prospective prediction — it is after-the-fact confirmation of publicly known data, performed in batch with four other predictions (WIN-035 through WIN-039), all registered the same day. The pattern indicates systematic data mining.

**4. No Dome Geometry Involvement**
Diameter (20,015 km), firmament height (9,086 km), sun altitude (5,733 km) — none of these parameters were used to derive 21,795 nT. The dome claims 'aetheric field geometry explains the African cell decay,' but provides no equations connecting geometry to rate or threshold. The claim is asserted, not derived.

**5. Cycle 1 Patches Mostly Applied**
The decider's proposed patches (ISS-150, ISS-198) mostly fixed the text. WMM2025 relationship is now clarified. However, ISS-198 (about the dome's claimed aetheric mechanism) was incomplete — the dome's actual mechanism claim is still not directly engaged with. This is a minor rhetorical gap, not a factual error.

**Verdict Confidence: 0.96** (very high, up from 0.95 in Cycle 1)
The slight increase reflects Cycle 1 patches being applied and the 6-day batch registration pattern now being explicitly documented in detail_evidence. The verdict ('Std Model Explains') is very solid. The only residual uncertainty (4%) reflects the possibility that future dome documents might provide an equation derivation from geometric parameters that we haven't yet seen — but no such derivation exists in the current repository.
comparison_to_cycle_1: Cycle 1 review was thorough and mostly accurate. Cycle 2 review confirms all major findings. Key differences: (1) Cycle 1 noted the decay rate discrepancy (25 vs 28-30 nT/yr) — patches applied, now consistent; (2) Cycle 1 identified the 6-day registration-to-confirmation gap as strong evidence — this is now prominent in detail_evidence; (3) Cycle 1 proposed engaging with the dome's claimed mechanism — this patch was partially applied but not fully. Cycle 2 adds: explicit notation of the batch pattern's implications (systematic data mining) and the 'information content near zero' argument, which is the strongest rhetorical weapon. The verdict holds unchanged.

key_passages: The SAA African cell has been decaying at ~28–30 nT/yr for decades
PROS-005 was registered 6 March 2026 and confirmed 12 March 2026 — a 6-day turnaround
Non-discriminating: both models extrapolate the same station data
The threshold of 21,795 nT was derived by extrapolating WIN-005's observed CHAOS-7 data
text_fingerprint.consistency_with_cycle_1: High. All major claims from Cycle 1 remain unchanged. The text has been incrementally improved (decay rate clarified to 28-30 throughout, WMM2025 relationship explained, timing argument highlighted). No contradictions detected.

citations_verified: WMM2025 link (https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/world-magnetic-model) — correct NOAA product page
citations_unchecked: CHAOS-7 (2000-2025 African cell 23,050 → 21,880 nT) — no DOI provided in WIN-035 text. Should cite: Finlay et al. (2020) or specific CHAOS-7 technical report.
WMM2025 release date (late 2024) — no specific citation in detail text

final_assessment.verdict_assessment: 'Std Model Explains' is correct and well-justified. The dome extrapolates the same station trends as WMM2025, offers no unique mechanism, and derives its threshold from observational curve-fitting rather than dome geometry. High confidence (0.96) that this verdict will survive future scrutiny unless the dome provides previously unseen geometric derivations.
final_assessment.strongest_point: The batch registration pattern (5 predictions registered same day, all confirmed within days using pre-existing data) is the most compelling evidence that this is post-hoc data mining, not prospective prediction. This argument, combined with the near-zero information content (threshold within established trend = certain confirmation), makes the case that WIN-035 is not a discriminating test of the dome model.
final_assessment.weakest_point: We could more explicitly engage with the dome's claimed mechanism ('aetheric field geometry explains African cell position-dependent decay') before refuting it. The proposed ISS-198 patch to do this was incomplete. As it stands, we say 'the dome offers no mechanism' when the dome does make a claim — we just don't show why that claim is empty. This is a minor rhetorical gap, not a factual error.
final_assessment.recommendation: Complete ISS-198 patch; make the batch pattern argument more prominent; expand finding statement to explicitly note near-zero information content. Otherwise, the WIN is in good shape for Cycle 2.
